audit_method: PubMed get_article_metadata spot-check + schema/effect-size/coherence rules; conservative HIGH only with PubMed ground truth; MED/LOW skipped for QUARANTINED-band reviews per protocol

findings.msg: PMID 31237646 is unrelated: PubMed article is 'Sigmoid Volvulus' (JAMA patient handout, doi:10.1001/jama.2019.2349). STOPDAPT-2 is the JAMA 2019 RCT — likely correct PMID is 31237671 or adjacent. Citation fabrication.
PMID 31237645 matches the trial (SMART-CHOICE JAMA 2019) but PubMed records the trial NCT as NCT02079194, not NCT02740283. NCT in extraction is wrong.

PMID 34453881 is unrelated: PubMed article is 'Live imaging of SARS-CoV-2 infection in mice' (Immunity 2021, doi:10.1016/j.immuni.2021.08.015). MASTER-DAPT is a 2021 NEJM PCI trial. Citation fabrication.

PMID 30792212 is unrelated: PubMed article is 'TP53 immunohistochemistry correlates with mutation status and clearance in decitabine-treated patients with myeloid malignancies' (Haematologica 2019). COAST-V is the ixekizumab AS trial. Citation fabrication.

PMID 30765211 is unrelated: PubMed article is 'The Use of Thromboelastography to Titrate Tranexamic Acid Therapy ... During VA-ECMO' (J Cardiothorac Vasc Anesth 2018, case report). Not the EF-15 TTFields NSCLC phase-2 trial. Citation fabrication.

PMID 25005549 is unrelated: PubMed article is 'The hospital anxiety and depression scale' (Occup Med 2014, review of HADS scale). CYD14 is the Capeding/Sabchareon 2014 dengue vaccine NEJM/Lancet trial. Citation fabrication.

PMID 42095072 is unrelated: PubMed article is 'Paleoproteomics of pottery food residues reveals plant cupin superfamily proteins preserved for thousands of years' (iScience 2026, doi:10.1016/j.isci.2026.115637). Has nothing to do with anifrolumab or SLE. PMID also exceeds typical late-2025 PMID range — high fabrication risk. Citation fabrication.

PMID 40021405 actually reports the iberdomide phase 2b SLE trial (NCT03161483; Ann Rheum Dis 2025, doi:10.1016/j.ard.2025.01.044). NCT03161483 = iberdomide, NOT dapirolizumab pegol. Extraction has both drug-name and trial-name (PHOENYCS-GO) wrong for this NCT. Drug-disease coherence (anti-CD40L claim) and identity both fail.

PMID 30215933 is unrelated: PubMed article is 'High-Dose vs. Standard-Dose Heparin for VTE Prophylaxis in Obese Patients'. MITRA-FR is the Obadia 2018 NEJM transcatheter mitral valve repair RCT. Citation fabrication.

PMID 39213188 is unrelated: PubMed article is 'Management of spinal trauma in pregnant patients: A systematic review'. MATTERHORN is the 2024 NEJM TEER-vs-surgery mitral repair RCT. Citation fabrication.

PMID 37222481 is unrelated: PubMed article is 'Point-of-care tests: the key to reducing antibiotic prescribing for respiratory tract infections in primary care?'. ONWARDS-2 is an icodec basal-insulin switch trial (Lancet 2023). Citation fabrication.

PMID 39839752 is unrelated: PubMed article is 'Mesenchymal Stem Cell-Sourced Exosomes as Potentially Novel Remedies for Severe Dry Eye Disease'. Not an efsitora alfa diabetes extension trial. Citation fabrication.

PMID 40749164 is a preclinical paper 'Preclinical advances in glofitamab combinations' (Blood 2025). It cites both NCT04408638 and NCT03467373 only as registered linkages, but is not the clinical-trial primary publication for GO40516 (mosunetuzumab+lenalidomide). Trial-publication mapping is wrong.

PMID 41371238 is the EPCORE FL-1 follicular-lymphoma trial (Lancet 2025; epcoritamab+R-len vs R-len). NCT05409066 in the PubMed record matches, but the trial is EPCORE FL-1, NOT EPCORE-DLBCL-1, and the population is relapsed/refractory follicular lymphoma, NOT DLBCL. Trial-name and disease both wrong.

PMID 42060297 says NCT04619797 is SKYSCRAPER-06 (advanced nonsquamous NSCLC, tira+atezo+chemo vs placebo+pembro+chemo; JAMA Oncol 2026, doi:10.1001/jamaoncol.2026.0818). The trial primary endpoint failed (PFS HR 1.27, OS HR 1.33). Extraction labels NCT04619797 as 'SKYSCRAPER-03' with HR 0.85 (0.71-1.02). Trial name AND effect direction wrong.

PMID 39855251 documents NCT04524871 as MORPHEUS-Liver (tira+atezo+bev HCC phase 1b-2, Lancet Oncol 2025). The extraction labels this as 'SKYSCRAPER-06', but SKYSCRAPER-06 per PMID 42060297 is NCT04619797 (NSCLC, failed). Trial-name collision; this NCT is MORPHEUS-Liver, not SKYSCRAPER-06.
PMID 41449142 is the SKYSCRAPER-08 ESCC trial publication. The PubMed-recorded NCT is NCT04540211, not NCT04746118. NCT in extraction is wrong.

Schema sanity: control arm cE=cN=105 (100% control event rate) is biologically implausible for an mitral-repair vs surgery composite-outcome trial. Either tE/tN/cE/cN swapped or control denominator equals events accidentally.

Schema sanity: cE=cN=18 (100% control event rate); HAVEN-1 reported annualized bleed counts in 18 prior-prophylaxis cohort, which is not a 0/1 binary 'event' denominator. Likely arm-aggregation error.

Schema sanity: cE=cN=83 (100% control event rate) for PROFOUND. PROFOUND (de Bono 2020 NEJM) reported rPFS HR 0.49 with imaging-progression events in <100% of comparator-arm patients; cE=cN denotes mis-extraction.
Schema sanity: cE=cN=40 (100% control event rate) for BAT azithromycin trial — exacerbation count is not denominator. Likely mis-extracted as total-N rather than event-N in control arm.
Schema sanity: cE=cN=70 (100% control event rate) — same pattern as BAT; likely event-count denominator confusion in extraction.

Plausibility: publishedHR=99.04 with CI [6.27, 1564] reflects OR derived from cE=0 (Feld 2015 NEJM ASTRAL-1 placebo arm: 0/116 SVR). The extreme OR is mathematically a continuity-correction artefact, not a usable effect size for pooling. Should be flagged for zero-cell handling or pooled on RD scale.
Plausibility: publishedHR=31.13 [4.4, 220] with cE=0 in control arm — same zero-cell artefact as ASTRAL-1. Continuity-correction-driven OR; not a stable estimate.

Plausibility: publishedHR=0.05 [0.003, 0.83] with tE=0/21. Very small-sample (n=21 challenge model) with zero-event treatment arm; OR is driven by continuity correction. Wide CI to 0.003 implies the small-sample-and-zero-cell handling, not a stable estimate.

Schema sanity: cE=cN=0 with publishedHR=null. Single-arm trial (cirrhotic GT1) — would be excluded from any comparator pool but is included in NMA review. Either remove from quantitative synthesis or flag explicitly as single-arm input.
Schema sanity: cE=cN=0 with publishedHR=null. STELLAR is single-arm phase 2 (TTFields+pem mesothelioma); cannot contribute to a comparator pool. Should be excluded from quantitative synthesis.
Schema sanity: cE=cN=0 with publishedHR=null — single-arm pediatric arm, same pattern as STELLAR/EXPEDITION-1.
Schema sanity: cE=cN=0 with publishedHR=null — single-arm Q4W dosing arm.
Schema sanity: cE=cN=0 with publishedHR=null — single-arm mild/moderate cohort.
Schema sanity: cE=cN=0 with publishedHR=null — single-arm; would be excluded from comparator pool.
Schema sanity: cE=cN=0 with publishedHR=null — single-arm; flag for exclusion.

Schema sanity: 7 of 10 trials have cE=cN=0 with publishedHR=null (all bispecific antibody single-arm/non-comparative trials: NP30179, EPCORE-NHL-1, EPCORE-NHL-3, GO29781, GO40516, ELM-1, ELM-2). Only 3 trials (STARGLO, EPCORE-DLBCL-1, OLYMPIA) are comparator-based. Pooling is structurally questionable; the review is dominated by single-arm response-rate evidence that cannot enter an NMA on HR.

Schema sanity: 8 of 9 trials have cE=cN=0 with publishedHR=null. Only MajesTEC-3 (NCT05083169) is comparator-based. Same structural problem as the lymphoma review — NMA on HR is not supportable from this evidence base.

Schema sanity: ALL 10 TKI-discontinuation trials have cE=cN=0 with publishedHR=null — these are single-arm treatment-free-remission cohort studies, not comparative trials. The review is entirely non-comparative; no NMA-relevant comparator HR available.

Plausibility/value: extracted publishedHR=1.17 [1.02, 1.34] labelled estimandType=RR. PMID 41363801 (NEJM 2025, doi:10.1056/NEJMoa2514663) reports PFS HR=0.17 [0.12, 0.23] favoring teclistamab+daratumumab. If extraction intends a response-rate ratio (ORR 89.0% vs 75.3%, RR≈1.18) the value is plausible; but the directionality vs PFS HR is opposite, and label/estimand conflation with the pooled HR endpoint is high-risk for the NMA. Confirm the extracted endpoint is response-rate, not PFS HR.

Field-name convention: field 'publishedHR' is used with estimandType=RR (ASAS20/40 response ratios), which is internally consistent for this review but conflates HR with RR at the field level. Recommend renaming or adding an explicit 'estimand_value_scale' tag. Same pattern affects DENGUE, LUPUS, HCV, INSULIN, HEMOPHILIA, BRONCHIECTASIS, IBD reviews.

review_verdicts.rationale: 3 HIGH citation/identity defects in 10 trials (STOPDAPT-2 wrong PMID, SMART-CHOICE wrong NCT, MASTER-DAPT wrong PMID)
1 HIGH (COAST-V wrong PMID); other 9 trials have null PMIDs which prevented spot-checks
1 HIGH (EF-15 wrong PMID); 2 single-arm with no comparator (STELLAR, EF-15)
1 HIGH (CYD14 wrong PMID); 1 MED zero-cell extreme effect (TV003-1)
2 HIGH defects including a PMID (42095072) that points to a paleoproteomics paper — strong fabrication signal; PHOENYCS-GO has wrong drug for the NCT

2 MED zero-cell extreme ORs (ASTRAL-1 OR=99, POLARIS-1 OR=31); 1 single-arm (EXPEDITION-1); identity spot-checks of PMID 26575258 and 29365309 reused across 2 trials each — acceptable since each paper covers both arms, but flag for review
2 HIGH wrong-PMID (ONWARDS-2, Q-WEEKLY-EXTEND) out of 5 testable PMIDs — 40% PMID failure rate on this review
2 HIGH wrong-PMID (MITRA-FR, MATTERHORN); 1 MED schema (MATTERHORN 100% control event)
1 MED schema (HAVEN-1 cE=cN=18); 4 single-arm trials; spot-checked PMIDs (28691557, 37003278, 38452197, 40608864) all matched
2 HIGH identity defects (GO40516 preclinical-paper PMID, EPCORE-DLBCL-1 actually EPCORE FL-1); 7/10 trials single-arm
3 HIGH identity defects in the SKYSCRAPER series (NCT04619797 is actually SKYSCRAPER-06 NSCLC not SKYSCRAPER-03; NCT04524871 is MORPHEUS-Liver not SKYSCRAPER-06; SKYSCRAPER-08 NCT wrong)
3 spot-checked PMIDs all matched (AFFIRM-AHF 33197395, IRONMAN 36347265, FAIR-HF 19920054); no schema or plausibility flags
All 10 trials are single-arm TFR cohorts (cE=cN=0) — NMA structurally not supportable from this evidence base
No PMIDs to spot-check; trial values plausible; reasonable comparator structure
2 MED schema (BAT, EMBRACE 100% control events)
No PMIDs to spot-check; trial values plausible; estimandType=RR with publishedHR field is internally consistent but conflated (see L01)
1 MED schema (PROFOUND cE=cN=83); 2 single-arm (TALAPRO-1, GALAHAD)
No PMIDs to verify against; SABR-COMET (NCT01446744) and STOMP (NCT03543189) values plausible vs published HRs
8/9 trials single-arm; MajesTEC-3 (the only comparator) shows extracted HR=1.17 versus the true PFS HR=0.17 — direction and value conflation likely (P02 finding)
QUARANTINED band; no HIGH-severity ground-truth-verifiable defects detected (per protocol, MED/LOW skipped)
QUARANTINED band; no HIGH-severity ground-truth-verifiable defects detected (per protocol, MED/LOW skipped)

key_patterns: Pattern 1 — PMID is a real PMID but points to unrelated paper (paleoproteomics, sigmoid volvulus, dry-eye exosomes, spinal trauma, etc.). Highest-yield fabrication signal; appears in ≥10 trials across 8 reviews.
Pattern 2 — High-numbered PMIDs (>40M) that exceed reasonable late-2025 issuance range AND point to unrelated content (42095072, 42060297). Synthetic PMID risk.

Pattern 3 — SKYSCRAPER trial-name collision: NCT04619797 is SKYSCRAPER-06 NSCLC (not -03), NCT04524871 is MORPHEUS-Liver (not -06), NCT04746118 is not SKYSCRAPER-08 (true NCT NCT04540211). Three-way name swap suggests batch-generated naming without per-trial verification.
Pattern 4 — 100% control-arm event rates (cE=cN) in 5 trials across 4 reviews — likely arm-aggregation extraction bug where total-N entered as event count.

Pattern 5 — 3 reviews are structurally non-comparative (CML_TFR 10/10, MM_BISPECIFIC 8/9, LYMPHOMA 7/10 single-arm) — NMA on HR not supportable; should be flagged at review-design level, not trial level.

Pattern 6 — MajesTEC-3 published PFS HR=0.17 but extracted value 1.17 (RR-scale). Same numeric digit-shift risk seen across other NMAs — verify whether all 'estimandType=RR' trials in this chunk are response-rate or were silently converted from time-to-event HRs.
Pattern 7 — Zero-cell continuity-correction artefacts (ASTRAL-1 OR=99, POLARIS-1 OR=31, TV003-1 OR=0.05) entered as if stable estimates.
